summary: Justin teaches the fundamental formulas for calculating Net Operating Income (NOI) and using the cap rate formula as a quick analysis tool. He demonstrates how knowing two of three variables (NOI, cap rate, price) allows investors to reverse-engineer deal feasibility and validate whether a seller's asking price is justified by market conditions.
takeaways: NOI = Gross Income + Other Income - Operating Expenses (fundamental formula)
Cap Rate = NOI ÷ Price (the three-piece puzzle formula)
If price is $30M and market cap rate is 4%, expect NOI around $1.2M ($30M × 0.04)
Use market cap rates from brokers as benchmarks to validate deals quickly
If calculated NOI doesn't match expected NOI based on price and cap rate, seller is asking too much
Example: $1.3M NOI ÷ $1.3M = 4.3% cap rate on $30M purchase
Reverse formula: Price = NOI ÷ Cap Rate (e.g., $1.3M ÷ 0.04 = $32.5M max price)

skill_candidates.change: Apply two-of-three cap rate puzzle to quickly validate asking prices against market conditions
skill_candidates.evidence: Justin demonstrates using known market cap rates (4%) and asking price ($30M) to back into expected NOI ($1.2M), then comparing to actual financials to assess if seller is overpriced—a time-efficient underwriting screening tool for deal sourcing workflow

title: Lesson4: Calculating NOI
